body.title: [sprint] Architect/editor split in the coding domain: strong planner + devstral-as-editor
body.intention: I intend that DS.0's builder is leveled to complete real tickets unaided by fixing the coding loop's interface, because the interface — not the 24B model — is what blocks it from editing.
body.description:
**Problem:** one 24B local model (devstral@Hex) is asked to orient + plan + serialize edits in a single ReAct stream. Two live DS.0 observe-runs (2026-07-04) made 0 Write/Edit *attempts* across 149 tool calls (47 Read/8 Bash, then 102 Read/12 Bash) — it never reaches an edit; it dies in orientation/planning. aider's architect mode splits the jobs: a strong 'architect' model resolves the task and emits plain-text instructions about which files to change and how; a cheap 'editor' model turns those instructions into a syntactically-correct diff/whole-file edit (aider 'editor-diff'/'editor-whole' formats).
**Proposed shape:** add an architect/editor mode to the coding domain (CodingDomain / BaseDomain.run). A capable planner (routed via the existing cost-router at the appropriate tier) produces a STRUCTURED edit-plan (prose: target files, the change per file, the why). That plan is handed to devstral as the EDITOR with a narrow 'produce the edit for THIS instruction' contract; editor output applies through the existing edit tools. The escalation walk + cost-router + bus/shim substrate are untouched — this changes only what the inner loop asks each model to do. Log the planner->editor handoff (it's an interface crossing).
**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/domains/base.py, domains/coding*.py, unseen_university/devices/inference/agentic_loop.py (needs an editor-only loop mode). MEDIUM+ inertia: agentic_loop.py + domains/base.py are the coding design-center — flag for pre-approval.
**Design rules:** device.py/shim design-center (the domain object owns the split, no standalone functions); log every interface crossing (planner->editor handoff); proof-on-close.
**Scope boundary:** IN — the architect/editor two-role flow, the planner->editor handoff, the editor edit-contract. OUT — the repo-map orientation surface (T-coding-repo-map-orientation) and the ACI viewer (T-coding-minion-aci-edit-centric).
**Test plan:** unit test — given a planner emitting a fixed edit-plan (target file + concrete change) and a stub editor, assert the editor is invoked with the plan's target+intent AND the APPLIED edit reflects that intent (pin the edit CONTENT against the plan, not merely that a mock ran — a mocked-invocation-only assertion passes on a hollow build; memory verify-divergence-before-collapsing). Behavioral red: the single-model (no-split) path never produces an edit-tool call for the same ticket. Empirical confirm-by lives in T-coding-redesign-observe-edits-then-close.
**Completion criteria:** the coding domain runs a two-role architect(planner)/editor(devstral) flow AND a test proves the editor produces an applied edit whose content matches the planner's intent, and that the split is what produces the edit call (single-model path does not).
**Rollback:** the split is behind a mode/flag on the coding domain; disabling it restores the single-model loop.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
